meaning: n.陦･雜ｳ迚ｩ,[譁勤ｳ評陦･隸ｭ,[謨ｰ]菴呵ｧ致t.陦･蜉ｩ,陦･雜ｳ
examples: There will be a written examination to complement the practical test.
The therapy can be a useful technique to complement traditional forms of psychotherapy.
We must aim to complement the rationality of the machine, rather than to compete with it.
Labour's boldest step has been to complement the internal market with an external one.
Microsoft is selling business software and entertainment hardware (Xboxes) to complement its storage.
